mission.title: Install and Configure Crossplane on Kubernetes
mission.description: Crossplane is a framework for building cloud native control planes without needing to write code. It allows you to orchestrate applications and infrastructure seamlessly across various environments.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Crossplane Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add crossplane https://charts.crossplane.io/master
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Crossplane using Helm
steps.description:
Install Crossplane in the `crossplane-system` namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install crossplane crossplane/crossplane --namespace crossplane-system --create-namespace --version 2.3.0-rc.0.39.g153a39424
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Crossplane pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane-system
```

steps.title: Configure Crossplane RBAC
steps.description:
Set up RBAC for Crossplane to ensure it has the necessary permissions.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/crossplane/crossplane/master/docs/getting-started/cluster-admin.yaml
```

steps.title: Set resource limits for Crossplane
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for Crossplane to optimize performance.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment crossplane -n crossplane-system --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall Crossplane from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall crossplane --namespace crossplane-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Crossplane CRDs
uninstall.description:
Delete all Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Crossplane.
```bash
kubectl delete crd --all
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Crossplane namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the `crossplane-system` namespace to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace crossplane-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of Crossplane before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane-system -o yaml > crossplane-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Crossplane
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Crossplane to the latest version available in the Helm repository.
```bash
helm upgrade crossplane crossplane/crossplane --namespace crossplane-system --version 2.3.0-rc.0.39.g153a39424
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Crossplane pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If Crossplane pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n crossplane-system
``` 
Common issues may include misconfiguration or insufficient resources. Adjust resource limits or review configuration files.

troubleshooting.title: Helm installation fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm installation fails, check for existing resources in the namespace.
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane-system
``` 
If resources exist, consider uninstalling them or using a different namespace.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC issues
troubleshooting.description:
If Crossplane cannot access certain resources, verify the RBAC configuration.
```bash
kubectl get clusterrolebindings | grep crossplane
``` 
Ensure that the necessary roles and bindings are correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are being killed due to OOMKilled, check the resource usage.
```bash
kubectl top pods -n crossplane-system
``` 
Consider increasing the memory limits in the deployment configuration.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Crossplane will have all pods running in the `crossplane-system` namespace without errors. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring all components are operational.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add crossplane https://charts.crossplane.io/master
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and Helm installed.
